description: PC GPU power profile during sustained inference workload (T-0615 perplexity rerun) on RTX 5090

workload.note: Same workload as T-0615.pc; the perplexity sweep provides a sustained, repeatable GPU load for power profiling.

power_w.load_inference_steady_note: Mean of last 4 samples once GPU util hit >90%; reflects sustained Blackwell prefill draw under TQ4_1S inference.
power_w.idle_post_steady_note: Mean of trailing 3 samples after the post-unload thermal/clock ramp-down.

gpu_util_pct.load_steady_note: Mean util once tensors finished uploading (samples 22-25).

temperature_c.thermal_envelope_note: Peak temp 42 C is well below RTX 5090 throttle threshold; cooling headroom is substantial under this short (~10 s) workload.

energy_estimate.method: Riemann sum over 1 Hz samples; load includes ramp-up + steady-state inference.

vs_idle_baseline.note: Sustained inference draws ~13.8x the idle power on the 5090.

vs_tdp_nominal.note: Peak draw (504 W) reaches 87.7% of the 575 W TDP; full TDP would require longer sustained throughput.

notes: C0 reference power profile for PC. P0 single-node, no compression beyond TQ4_1S quantization.

Workload duration was short (~10 s of true inference) because perplexity over 20 chunks at c=512 is a small sweep; future T-0616 reruns at c=2048 or --chunks 100 will produce a longer steady-state envelope.
Power trace shows a clear three-phase shape: idle pre (~31 W), tensor upload ramp (50-150 W), inference plateau (266-504 W with mean ~429 W in the last 4 samples).
Sample at 15:36:20 (325.57 W, 1021 MiB VRAM) captures the model unload boundary; not representative of either inference or true idle.
VRAM peak of 31,351 MiB (96.2% of 32,606) confirms the C0 PC budget is tight; any longer-context or larger-batch C1-C7 cell will need TriAttention KV reduction to fit.
Companion result: results/t0615_pc_perplexity.json (workload that sourced this trace).
